strands.title: Task Structure and Scope
strands.content: A comprehensive code review of the pichay codebase has been requested. The scope is well-defined: 7 source files in src/pichay/ (proxy.py, pager.py, eval.py, replay.py, analyzer.py, trimmer.py, __main__.py) and multiple test files in tests/ directory. The review must assess three dimensions for source files (code quality, potential bugs/edge cases, cross-module consistency) and two dimensions for test files (coverage gaps, false-pass risks). Output must be specific (line numbers, file paths) and formatted to review.md in project root. This is a READ-and-ASSESS task, not a refactor-and-fix task.

key_claims.text: The review scope includes exactly 7 source files and test files in tests/
The review must produce findings with specific line numbers and file paths
Five distinct assessment dimensions are required (code quality, bugs/edge cases, consistency, coverage, false passes)

strands.title: Assessment Dimensions and Criteria
strands.content: The review must evaluate source code across: (1) Code quality issues including unused imports, dead code, and type errors; (2) Potential bugs and unhandled edge cases; (3) Cross-module consistency in naming conventions and error handling patterns. For tests, the review must identify: (4) Missing coverage for important code paths; (5) Tests that may produce false positives (testing incorrect behavior or wrong assertions). These dimensions suggest the review should examine both surface-level code hygiene and deeper logical soundness, both in implementation and validation.

key_claims.text: Code quality assessment includes unused imports, dead code, and type errors
Cross-module consistency assessment focuses on naming conventions and error handling patterns
Test review must identify both coverage gaps and false-positive risks

strands.title: Execution Readiness and Dependencies
strands.content: This task requires actual file I/O access to the pichay codebase. The request assumes: (a) the codebase exists at a known path or context; (b) all 7 source files and test files are readable; (c) the project root location is identifiable for writing review.md. The task is sequential in nature — files must be read before assessment, and all assessments must be completed before writing the consolidated review. No prior analysis context is available; this is a cold start on unknown code.

key_claims.text: The task requires direct file I/O access to src/pichay/ and tests/ directories
No prior context about pichay codebase content or structure is available
The assessment must be independent and specific to actual code content

declared_losses.what_was_lost: No actual code analysis has been performed yet; this is purely meta-level task parsing
declared_losses.why: The tensor was initialized with only the user request, not with actual file contents. Code review requires reading files first.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Details about pichay's domain, purpose, architecture, and design intent
declared_losses.why: Only a scope list was provided; no context about what pichay does or how it's supposed to work. This context would inform what constitutes a real bug vs. intentional design.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Specific tool/framework dependencies that might affect what constitutes a 'code quality issue'
declared_losses.why: The files are Python but no indication of Python version, linting rules, type-checking tools (mypy, pyright), or project standards were provided.

open_questions: Are all 7 source files and test files accessible and readable from the current execution environment?
What is the intended purpose and architecture of the pichay codebase? (Required context for assessing design consistency.)
What are the project's established coding standards, linting rules, and type-checking requirements?
What Python version and dependencies does pichay target? (Affects interpretation of some code quality issues.)
Are there existing tests or CI/CD configurations that indicate expected coverage thresholds?
Should the review treat missing docstrings or comments as code quality issues?
How deep should edge-case analysis go? (All possible inputs, or only realistic/documented scenarios?)
instructions_for_next:
[
  "NEXT CYCLE: Attempt to read all 7 source files from src/pichay/. For each file, extract: imports, function/class definitions, control flow complexity, error handling patterns, and any obvious code smells.",
  "Then read all test files from tests/. Extract: test function names, what they test, assertions used, mocking patterns, and any apparent coverage gaps.",
  "During reading, collect evidence for each of the 5 assessment dimensions. Store findings with specific line references.",
  "After collection, synthesize findings into themes and inconsistencies. Rank by severity (critical bug, design issue, hygiene issue).",
  "Finally, write review.md with structured sections: (A) Source Code Findings, (B) Test Coverage Analysis, (C) Cross-Module Consistency Issues, (D) Summary & Recommendations.",
  "Flag any read errors or inaccessible files immediately; do not assume content."
]
